articles.author: NA
articles.headline: Landmine casualties hit four-year high as treaty setbacks deepen
articles.text:
Civilian deaths and injuries from landmines and explosive remnants of war have risen to a four-year high, according to the Landmine Monitor 2025 report. The report documents 6,279 casualties in 2024, with children making up a large proportion.
Loren Persi of the Impact Team noted that 90% of casualties were civilians.

TREATY SETBACKS
The 1997 Mine Ban Treaty faces significant challenges. Estonia, Finland, Latvia, Lithuania, and Poland are moving to withdraw because of security concerns following Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
Ukraine has suggested it can suspend treaty obligations during wartime — a position the Monitor disputes.
The report also cites potential new Ukrainian mine use in 2024–2025.

GLOBAL USE AND CONTAMINATION
Myanmar and Russia used mines extensively. Cambodia and Thailand face allegations of cross-border mine deployment.
Mine contamination affects at least 57 countries. Seven remain heavily contaminated: Afghanistan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cambodia, Ethiopia, Iraq, Türkiye, and Ukraine.
Oman completed its clearance obligations in 2025.

FUNDING CRISIS
Mine-action programs face severe funding shortages. The U.S. imposed a sector-wide funding freeze in 2025, causing shutdowns in Afghanistan, Yemen, Colombia, Tajikistan, and Zimbabwe.
Victim assistance programs suffered steep declines.

CALL FOR ACTION
Researchers warn that without renewed political and financial commitments, mine contamination may outpace clearance efforts.

articles.author: Jimmy Rustling
articles.headline: Why Your Office Environment is Secretly Sabotaging Your Success
articles.text:
The article argues that poorly maintained office environments harm productivity, morale, and employee well-being. It describes how clutter, odors, dust, and neglected shared spaces create psychological and health burdens.
The author recounts visiting a client’s office that felt "heavy and stale," using it to illustrate how workplace neglect can reduce concentration and elevate stress.

THE HIDDEN COST OF A NEGLECTED WORKSPACE
Untidy or unsanitary work environments can increase stress levels by up to 40%, according to the article. Visual clutter, unpleasant smells, and dirty surfaces force the brain to expend energy that should support work tasks.
The author claims that cleaning interventions improved morale and productivity at companies he consulted for.

PSYCHOLOGY OF CLEANLINESS
Clean spaces promote feelings of control, reduce anxiety, and create a more inviting workplace. In contrast, unsanitary bathrooms, grimy windows, malfunctioning equipment, and dirty kitchens undermine morale.

KEY FACTORS THAT IMPACT WORKPLACE FEELING
- Air quality and ventilation
- Sanitization of high-touch surfaces
- Clean bathrooms and kitchen areas
- Natural light and clean windows

RIPPLE EFFECT ON CULTURE
Improved cleanliness encourages employees to maintain their individual spaces, reduces sick leave, and boosts team collaboration.
The article cites a tech startup that suffered high absenteeism and retention issues until it invested in structured cleaning practices.

CONCLUSION
The author recommends outsourcing cleaning services to ensure consistent upkeep and argues that a clean environment is foundational to workplace success.

articles.author: Jennifer Jacobs, James LaPorta
articles.headline: Top officials present Trump with military options for Venezuela in the coming days
articles.text:
Senior military officials briefed President Trump on updated options for potential operations in Venezuela, including possible land strikes. No decision has been finalized.
Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Joint Chiefs Chairman Dan Caine, and other senior officials presented the options at the White House. The Pentagon and White House declined to comment.
The intelligence community contributed information to support planning. DNI Tulsi Gabbard was absent due to travel for an overseas trip.

U.S. MILITARY POSITIONING
The USS Gerald Ford carrier strike group entered U.S. Southern Command’s area of responsibility earlier this week. Southern Command oversees military operations in the Caribbean and South America.
The carrier joins destroyers, warplanes, and special operations units already positioned in the region.

COUNTERNARCOTICS STRIKES
Over the last two months, the U.S. military has conducted strikes against at least 21 vessels allegedly transporting drugs to the United States. About 80 suspected smugglers have been killed.
At a defense summit in Indiana, Hegseth warned foreign traffickers: “We will find you and we will kill you.”

NEXT STEPS
Two sources said operational decisions could be made in the coming days.

articles.author: Andrew Dalton
articles.headline: ‘Zootopia 2' roars to record-setting global box office with $556M opening
articles.text:
“Zootopia 2” delivered a record-setting global box office debut, earning $96 million in North America over the weekend and $156 million across the five-day Thanksgiving period. Globally, the animated sequel brought in $556 million since its Wednesday opening.
This marks the highest international opening ever for an animated film and the fourth-highest global debut for any movie.

HOLIDAY BOX OFFICE BOOST
Universal’s “Wicked: For Good” also performed strongly, earning $62.8 million domestically in its second weekend and reaching $393 million internationally.
Together, the PG-rated sequels brought the Thanksgiving weekend total to $290 million — a bright spot in an otherwise challenging year for theaters.

LOOKING AHEAD
Upcoming releases such as “Avatar: Fire and Ash” and “Five Nights at Freddy’s 2” may determine whether 2025 finishes as a strong box office year or remains below industry expectations.

articles.author: Kyndall Cunningham
articles.headline: Gen Z is changing what status symbols look like — From Trader Joe’s totes to Starbucks Bearista cups, low-end items carry more meaning in the affordability era
articles.text:
In an era of economic uncertainty, Gen Z is embracing inexpensive novelty items as modern status symbols. Products like Starbucks' viral “Bearista” tumbler, Trader Joe’s micro tote bags, and Owala water bottles have sparked massive lines, resale markets, and intense social-media frenzy.
The Bearista cup, a teddy-bear-shaped $30 tumbler originally from South Korea, sold out quickly across U.S. stores. Reports emerged of customers arguing with staff and fighting each other to secure one. Starbucks apologized but did not confirm whether it would restock the item.
Many who acquired the cup posted their successes online, while resellers listed them at inflated prices.

VIRAL CONSUMER CULTURE
This trend reflects a broader shift where small, affordable items generate more online clout than luxury goods. Gen Z consumers often value uniqueness, community trends, and participation in shared online experiences.
While the fascination with scarce trinkets is not new, social media has amplified the intensity and speed of these viral waves.
The phenomenon extends to other limited or difficult-to-obtain merchandise, reinforcing how value is increasingly defined by cultural moment rather than intrinsic price.

AUTHOR BACKGROUND
Kyndall Cunningham writes about cultural and social trends influencing perceptions of gender, image, relationships, and consumption.

articles.author: Advaita Suresh
articles.headline: Terrifying moment: Fan grabs Ariana Grande on red carpet, Co-Star Cynthia Erivo steps in
articles.text:
At the Wicked: For Good premiere in Singapore, a fan breached security and grabbed Ariana Grande on the red carpet. Grande appeared shocked as Cynthia Erivo stepped between her and the intruder.

CYNTHIA ERIVO PRAISED
Witnesses reported that Erivo immediately intervened to protect Grande while security restrained the man. Fans expressed outrage at the slow security response, and video clips went viral online.

INTRUDER'S HISTORY
Social media identified the man as “Pyjama Man,” known for repeatedly disrupting celebrity events. He has previously interfered at concerts by Katy Perry, The Chainsmokers, and The Weeknd.

FAN REACTION
Many fans criticized security, while others celebrated Erivo’s quick response. Several comments highlighted Grande’s past trauma from the 2017 Manchester bombing.

ABOUT THE FILM
Directed by Jon M. Chu, Wicked: For Good stars Ariana Grande as Glinda and Cynthia Erivo as Elphaba. It is set to premiere in Indian theaters November 21.

articles.author: Unknown
articles.headline: SNORE OFF — If you snore you could be THREE TIMES more likely to die of coronavirus, docs warn
articles.text:
Experts are warning that people with obstructive sleep apnea — a condition involving repeated airway blockage during sleep — may face a significantly higher risk of severe outcomes or death from COVID-19.
Researchers from the University of Warwick reviewed multiple studies linking sleep apnea to worse COVID-19 outcomes. Their analysis found that patients with the condition were up to three times more likely to require hospitalization or die.

RISK FACTORS
Sleep apnea often occurs alongside diabetes, hypertension, and obesity — the same health conditions associated with increased COVID-19 risk.
One large study found hospitalized diabetic patients with diagnosed sleep apnea had a 2.8 times greater risk of dying within seven days.

UNDERDIAGNOSIS CONCERNS
As many as 85% of cases are believed to be undetected. With obesity rates rising, researchers warn that sleep apnea prevalence may be increasing as well.

SCIENTIFIC CONTEXT
Researchers emphasized that more data is needed to determine whether sleep apnea patients should be categorized as clinically vulnerable.
They recommend that diagnosed individuals strictly follow their treatment plans and adopt extra COVID-19 precautions such as masking, distancing, and early testing.
